gpu: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3080
notes:
Tried different proton versions, both GE, and steam's vanilla - all to same effect.
No window appears. The crash occurs very early in the process: a game thread repeatedly triggers Linux SIGSYS (seccomp) events, then immediately hits an access violation inside Client.exe.

gpu: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 SUPER
notes: Initially was able to preload for the playtest and open it up, but the playtest hadn't begun yet. After they updated it, it just never opens at all. Tested on proton-experimental and 10.0-3 as I saw others in this forum using that version. Reinstalled to no avail, was able to open it and play just fine on my Win10 drive. Hopefully they fix this up, but right now I'm guessing it won't work on Linux until launch (if we're lucky)

gpu: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3070 Ti

Idles for a while while it says "running" and then closes itself. Didn't work on my friends machine either.

Bonus: It created an 11GB Proton log hahahahah
